checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output JSON against the source PDF. Every piece of text in the output, including the title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', the date 'January 8, 2025', the legend items, and the sources section, is directly derived from the source document. There are no fabricated sentences or paragraphs.

The source document does not contain any phone numbers, email addresses, mailing addresses, or URLs. Correspondingly, the output JSON does not contain any such information. No contact information was fabricated.

The source document consists of a single page containing a map, a title, a date, a legend, a logo, and source information. All these components are represented in the output JSON. The textual information is provided in heading, list, and paragraph types, while the graphical elements (map and logo) are included as image types. No entire section or page is missing.
The source document does not contain any tables. This check is not applicable, but no data-loss in a tabular format occurred.

While individual county names appearing as labels on the map and the scale bar text are not extracted as separate text elements, they are part of the map image itself, which is standard for this type of conversion. There are no significant omissions of the document's informative text.

The source attribution section also perfectly mirrors the text at the bottom left of the PDF. No meaning-altering changes were found.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the source document and the converted output. The text present in the document (titles, legend labels, and source information) appears exactly once in the output. The county labels shown in the OCR block are part of the map image and are correctly handled as part of the image element rather than being duplicated as separate text blocks.
The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a standard structure. The main title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County' is an H1, and the date 'January 8, 2025' is an H2. There are no skipped levels.
The source document contains no tabular data. The legend information is correctly interpreted as lists.

The reading order in the output follows a logical, top-down flow: Title -> Date -> Legend -> Sources -> Map Image -> Logo. While the visual layout has the map central and legend/sources/logo at the bottom, the converted order is optimized for accessibility and flows logically.

The legend section 'Parcel Updates:' is correctly converted into list structures. Although it is split into two separate list elements (one for headers and one for the data items), the semantic meaning and grouping of the legend items are preserved.
There are no links in the source document, and none were incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the logical reading order of the source PDF. The title, date, legend (converted to a list), and sources are presented in the same sequence as the source document. While the layout is simplified for a web-friendly and accessible format (e.g., transforming the map legend into a bulleted list), the organization of information remains consistent with the source's intent. The inclusion of an extra header 'Map Imagery Logo Imagery F810' is an acceptable addition for accessibility/metadata.
The source document contains no tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are present.

The source PDF contains meaningful images that are essential to the document's purpose: a map of North Carolina counties (with data coloring), a north arrow, a scale bar, and the NCGICC logo. In the rendered HTML screenshot, these images are not visually present. Instead, there are broken image/placeholder icons followed by alternative text descriptions. The map itself is the primary content of the document, and its absence constitutes a significant visual and functional failure in the conversion.
checks.b_evidence_source: The map of North Carolina, the north arrow, the scale bar, and the NCGICC logo shown on page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: The images are absent, represented only by alt text: "A map of North Carolina counties colored according to the status of their parcel updates." and "NCGICC logo". The scale bar and north arrow are missing entirely with no alt text.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main body of the document and footer area of page 1.

checks.a_analysis: No body content, paragraphs, or lists are repeated in the HTML output.
